channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Singapore Blocks Social Media Posts Inciting Racial Discord Against Indians

media:content.media:description: Singapore has long positioned its multicultural model as foundational to its stability and identity

item.description: Singapore has long positioned its multicultural model - enshrined in law and national policy - as foundational to its stability and identity.
item.content:encoded: Singapore has long positioned its multicultural model - enshrined in law and national policy - as foundational to its stability and identity.

item.title: India, Nepal Launch Cross Border Digital Payments, 84 Projects Handed Over

media:content.media:description: S Jaishankar described the talks as detailed and substantive

item.description: In a moment of both humanitarian and diplomatic significance, S Jaishankar virtually handed over 72 health facilities and 12 cultural heritage projects reconstructed with Indian development assistance following the devastating 2015 earthquake that killed nearly 9,000 people in Nepal.
item.content:encoded: In a moment of both humanitarian and diplomatic significance, S Jaishankar virtually handed over 72 health facilities and 12 cultural heritage projects reconstructed with Indian development assistance following the devastating 2015 earthquake that killed nearly 9,000 people in Nepal.

item.title: No Women In US Navy Promotion List Raises Fears Of Career Ceiling

media:content.media:description: Pentagon has not offered any rationale on why they were removed from the promotion list.

item.description: The Navy had selected 31 sailors to promote from the rank of captain to one-star admiral, but Hegseth recently intervened to strike nine people from the list, including three women and two Black men, according to a defence official.
item.content:encoded: The Navy had selected 31 sailors to promote from the rank of captain to one-star admiral, but Hegseth recently intervened to strike nine people from the list, including three women and two Black men, according to a defence official.

item.title: 7-Month-Old Palestinian Baby Killed After Israeli Troops Shoot At Family Car

media:content.media:description: According to the military, troops "perceived a vehicle accelerating toward them"

item.description: Abu Haikal said the family was travelling with their 11-year-old son and his mother when the shooting took place. He rejected any suggestion that the soldiers could have mistaken the vehicle's occupants.
item.content:encoded: Abu Haikal said the family was travelling with their 11-year-old son and his mother when the shooting took place. He rejected any suggestion that the soldiers could have mistaken the vehicle's occupants.

item.title: Israeli Forces Kill Palestinian Baby In Occupied West Bank

media:content.media:description: Israeli soldiers accused of harming Palestinians are rarely penalised.

item.description: The official Palestinian news agency WAFA said the baby was critically wounded after being struck in the face by the same bullet that injured his mother.
item.content:encoded: The official Palestinian news agency WAFA said the baby was critically wounded after being struck in the face by the same bullet that injured his mother.

item.title: US Iran War Live Updates: Kuwait Responds To Missile, Drone Attacks, Sirens Sounded In Bahrain

media:content.media:description: US Iran War Live Updates

item.description: US Iran War Live Updates:United States military officials said Iran launched seven ballistic missiles toward neighboring Gulf nations of Kuwait and Bahrain.
item.content:encoded: US Iran War Live Updates:United States military officials said Iran launched seven ballistic missiles toward neighboring Gulf nations of Kuwait and Bahrain.

item.title: Why Ivanka Trump, Jared Kushner's Albania Resort Project Is Facing Protests

media:content.media:description: For nearly a week, protests have been growing in Albania against the project.

item.description: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama downplayed concerns Friday raised by protesters opposed to a planned tourism complex linked to US President Donald Trump's family, saying...
item.content:encoded: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama downplayed concerns Friday raised by protesters opposed to a planned tourism complex linked to US President Donald Trump's family, saying...

item.title: Norway Crown Princess Put On Waitlist For Lung Transplant

media:content.media:description: While awaiting the operation, the crown princess will not be able to carry out her official duties.

item.description: Mette-Marit, 52, was diagnosed in 2018 with a rare form of pulmonary fibrosis that causes breathing difficulties, which has repeatedly forced her to take sick leave or scale back her official duties.
item.content:encoded: Mette-Marit, 52, was diagnosed in 2018 with a rare form of pulmonary fibrosis that causes breathing difficulties, which has repeatedly forced her to take sick leave or scale back her official duties.

item.title: 'Thought I Would Perish': Everest Survivor Dawa Sherpa Recounts Ordeal

media:content.media:description: Dawa Sherpa survived on a few chocolates and snacks he found in his pockets.

item.description: Dawa Sherpa, 57, disappeared in brutal conditions on the upper slopes of the world's tallest mountain on May 30 during one of the final climbs of the spring season.
item.content:encoded: Dawa Sherpa, 57, disappeared in brutal conditions on the upper slopes of the world's tallest mountain on May 30 during one of the final climbs of the spring season.

item.title: Ebola's Hidden Link To Gold Mining: Why Scientists Are Raising Alarm In Congo

media:content.media:description: Congo is home to one of the world's largest rainforest systems (File)

item.description: Scientists believe Ebola viruses naturally exist in some wild animals, with bats considered one of the likely hosts.
item.content:encoded: Scientists believe Ebola viruses naturally exist in some wild animals, with bats considered one of the likely hosts.

item.title: Russian Satellites Are Jamming GPS Signals Across Europe, Warn Scientists

media:content.media:description: Experts claimed that these incidents reflect a growing trend of electronic warfare (Representational)

item.description: Scientists tracking the disruptions claimed that the incidents highlight how space-based technology is becoming a new battleground amid rising geopolitical tensions.
item.content:encoded: Scientists tracking the disruptions claimed that the incidents highlight how space-based technology is becoming a new battleground amid rising geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Showcases New Warship Ahead Of Xi Jinping's Visit

media:content.media:description: Kang Kon is the second of two destroyers North Korea unveiled last year.

item.description: Kim Jong Un called for "rapidly developing" naval forces capable of playing a larger role in the country's nuclear deterrent and dealing a "deadly blow at the enemy".
item.content:encoded: Kim Jong Un called for "rapidly developing" naval forces capable of playing a larger role in the country's nuclear deterrent and dealing a "deadly blow at the enemy".

item.title: Russia Says Shot Down 25 Drones In Saint Petersburg Region

media:content.media:description: Moscow and Kyiv have intensified drone strikes on each other in recent months.

item.description: Russian forces intercepted 25 drones near Saint Petersburg, where Moscow is hosting its flagship economic forum, the regional...
item.content:encoded: Russian forces intercepted 25 drones near Saint Petersburg, where Moscow is hosting its flagship economic forum, the regional...

item.title: Trump Calls For Military To Accelerate AI Use While Protecting Americans

media:content.media:description: The Defense Department has already been accelerating its use of AI in recent years.

item.description: The memo comes at a time of growing anxiety over AI in American society, from replacing people's jobs to helping to identify targets on the battlefield.
item.content:encoded: The memo comes at a time of growing anxiety over AI in American society, from replacing people's jobs to helping to identify targets on the battlefield.

item.title: Trump-Run Kennedy Center Took $2 Million From "Fake CIA Operative", Claims Lawsuit

media:content.media:description: Trump had added his name before Kennedy's on the facade of the building

item.description: The lawsuit claimed Srivastava had agreed to pay $10 million to sponsor an area in the Kennedy Center of which he had already donated $2 million.
item.content:encoded: The lawsuit claimed Srivastava had agreed to pay $10 million to sponsor an area in the Kennedy Center of which he had already donated $2 million.

item.title: US Adds $38 Million For Ebola As Health Body Warns Outbreak Could Match 2014

media:content.media:description: The CDC published on Friday three official scientific reports on the outbreak.

item.description: The U.S. said on Friday it would give an additional $38 million in funding for Ebola response efforts, as the U.S. CDC warned that
item.content:encoded: The U.S. said on Friday it would give an additional $38 million in funding for Ebola response efforts, as the U.S. CDC warned that

item.title: Bar Owner Faces New Charge Over Swiss Ski Resort Fire That Killed 41

media:content.media:description: Most of those killed were teenagers, many aged 16 and under.

item.description: A co-owner of the Swiss bar ravaged by a deadly fire was charged with forgery on Friday, adding to the numerous charges she and her husband already face over the...
item.content:encoded: A co-owner of the Swiss bar ravaged by a deadly fire was charged with forgery on Friday, adding to the numerous charges she and her husband already face over the...

item.title: 'We Come Without Past Baggage': Nepal Foreign Minister To NDTV On Gen Z Support

media:content.media:description: Shisir Khanal is on a three-day visit to India

item.description: On his maiden visit to New Delhi, Shisir Khanal signalled a decisive reset in bilateral relations, with energy, connectivity and digital cooperation topping the agenda.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1109488/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>On his maiden visit to New Delhi, Shisir Khanal signalled a decisive reset in bilateral relations, with energy, connectivity and digital cooperation topping the agenda.

item.title: "If He Wants A Deal...": Mojtaba Khamenei's Aide's "Test Of Trust" For Trump

media:content.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei's aide said that "the negotiations are at a deadlock"

item.description: He described the demand as a confidence-building measure and said Iran views the money as its own funds rather than a concession from the US.
item.content:encoded: He described the demand as a confidence-building measure and said Iran views the money as its own funds rather than a concession from the US.

item.title: Zelenskyy's Letter To Putin Was Intended For Other Ears

media:content.media:description: Zelenskyy has for months been repeating his call for a ceasefire, only for Putin to dismiss them.

item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's open letter offering facetoface peace talks to end the war in Ukraine was addressed to Russian President Vladimir Putin but
item.content:encoded: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's open letter offering facetoface peace talks to end the war in Ukraine was addressed to Russian President Vladimir Putin but
